text.title: API-First Development Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding API-First Development. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: OpenAPI 3.1 YAML is committed before any handler code; if the spec changes mid-feature, regenerate stubs and re-run contract tests in the same PR.

rationale.source: FIRST.org API design literature + OpenAPI spec v3.1.0

rule.rationale: Code-first APIs drift from documentation within weeks; locking the contract as the first commit prevents the drift.

rule.statement: Every operation carries a camelCase operationId; blank or duplicate operationIds are rejected.
rule.rationale: SDK generators and Prism routers key on operationId; blanks produce conflicting function names that break generated clients.

rule.statement: Nullable fields use type: ['string', 'null']; nullable: true (OAS 3.0 syntax) is rejected.
rule.rationale: OAS 3.1 aligns with JSON Schema 2020-12; the 3.0 nullable: true pattern produces incorrect validation in 3.1-aware tools.

rule.statement: All 4xx/5xx responses reference a single #/components/responses/Error; per-endpoint ad-hoc error shapes are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Consumers cannot write a single error handler when each endpoint returns a different error shape.

rule.statement: Prism mock server is deployed and shared with the consumer team before backend implementation begins.
rule.rationale: Mock-first lets frontend agents start immediately; without it the spec is written but never exercised.

rule.statement: Spectral lint passes as a pre-merge CI gate; lint errors that break mock-server routing block the PR.
rule.rationale: Without a CI gate, lint regressions ship and the spec degrades into documentation.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-api-first-development.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
